question_text: Two children sit on a seesaw balanced on a central pivot. Child A (mass 30 kg) sits 1.5 m from the pivot. Child B (mass 40 kg) sits 1.2 m from the pivot on the opposite side. Which child causes a larger moment about the pivot? (Take g = 10 N/kg)

options.label: A
options.text: Child A, because they have a smaller mass

options.label: B
options.text: Child A, because they sit further from the pivot

options.label: C
options.text: Child B, because they have a larger mass

options.label: D
options.text: Both children produce the same moment

correct_answer: B) Child A, because they sit further from the pivot
explanation: Child A's moment = 30 × 10 × 1.5 = 450 N m. Child B's moment = 40 × 10 × 1.2 = 480 N m. Actually Child B has the larger moment because although Child A is further away, the mass difference (40 kg vs 30 kg) creates a larger overall turning effect.
